# 🏛 Governance

## Project purpose

The EU AI Act Toolkit helps SMEs with readiness work, screening, documentation support, and escalation for higher-attention use cases.

## Maintainer role

Maintainers keep the project practical, conservative, and current with official source notes.

## Contributor role

Contributors improve templates, examples, docs, Pages content, and source notes without adding confidential data.

## Decision principles

- practical over academic
- plain English
- SME-friendly
- source-aware
- legal overclaiming is not allowed

## Content quality principles

- keep files usable
- keep wording cautious
- keep examples fictional or sample only
- keep disclaimers visible where needed

## Source update process

- check official EU sources before changing legal wording
- update source notes when assumptions change
- record changes in the changelog

## Release process

- merge through pull requests
- run the quality script
- update release notes where relevant

## Issue and PR triage

- label by topic and impact
- route sensitive items to the security path
- close or edit content that creates risk or confusion

## Legal overclaiming policy

Do not say the toolkit guarantees compliance.
Prefer readiness, screening, starting point, documentation support, and escalation.
